chunks.text: The Team for Research in Ubiquitous Secure Technology (TRUST) Education TRUST is focused on the development of cybersecurity science and technology that will radically transform the ability of organizations to design, build, and operate trustworthy i

ations to design, build, and operate trustworthy information systems for the nation's critical infrastructure. TRUST education focuses on integrating trustworthy technologies, systems, and policy into learning opportunities for High school, undergrad

learning opportunities for High school, undergraduate, and graduate students and junior faculty. OBJECTIVES Education is a core value for all TRUST initiatives. Our goal is to: Increase the participation of underrepresented students in undergraduate

tion of underrepresented students in undergraduate and graduate research in the fields of security, privacy, and trustworthy systems; Partner with educational institutions serving under-represented populations to engage students from diverse backgrou

pulations to engage students from diverse backgrounds and to prepare these students for careers in computer science and engineering; Engage the public in understanding the intersection of privacy and security with technology choices; Develop educatio

security with technology choices; Develop educational tools and modules for teaching courses on cyber security and privacy. TRUST education initiatives are focused on high school students, undergraduate students, graduate students, post docs, and fac
te students, graduate students, post docs, and faculty. Education Overview Pre-College Undergraduate Graduate Post Docs / Faculty
